title: Generating New Data After Training
concept: Once trained, a VAE can generate brand new samples by skipping the encoder entirely and feeding random latent vectors directly to the decoder.

exercises.prompt: After a VAE finishes training, what does the DECODER already know how to do?

options.text: Turn a latent vector (a list of numbers) into a realistic output like an image

exercises.explain: The decoder's whole job during training was to learn how to turn latent vectors into realistic outputs. Once trained, it can do this for ANY latent vector, not just ones from real images.

cards.title: No Input Needed!
cards.body: What if you could create a brand new face, drawing, or song... without ever showing the AI a starting picture? With a trained VAE, you can! Just skip straight to the decoder and hand it some random numbers. Watch what comes out.

cards.title: Generating New Data
cards.body: Once a VAE is trained, you don't need the encoder anymore. You can pick a random point in the latent space (just a list of random numbers) and feed it straight into the decoder. The decoder turns that random point into a brand new, never-seen-before output, like a new face or digit.

cards.title: The Recipe Machine
cards.body: Imagine the encoder learned to turn any dish into a 'recipe code,' and the decoder learned to turn any recipe code back into a dish. Once the decoder is a master chef, you don't need real dishes anymore. Just write a NEW random recipe code, hand it to the decoder-chef, and it cooks up a brand new dish nobody has ever tasted!

cards.title: New Faces From Nothing
cards.body: A VAE trained on thousands of face photos learns a latent space where similar faces sit close together. After training, you pick a random spot in that space (say, numbers like [0.2, -1.3, 0.7...]) and feed it to the decoder. Out comes a completely new face that was never in the training data!

cards.title: Myth: You Always Need the Encoder
cards.body: Myth: To generate anything, the AI must first encode a real example. Truth: Once trained, the encoder's job is done! Generation only needs the decoder plus a random latent vector. The encoder was just a training tool to teach the decoder its skills.

cards.title: Training vs. Generating
cards.body: During training, both halves work together. During generation, only the decoder is used, starting from random numbers instead of a real image.

left.text: Real image → Encoder → Latent vector → Decoder → Rebuilt image

right.text: Random numbers → Decoder → Brand new image

exercises.prompt: To generate a brand new image after training, what do you feed into the decoder?

options.text: A real photo from the training set

exercises.explain: The decoder needs a starting point in latent space. After training, we can just pick random numbers instead of using the encoder on a real image.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The encoder is required every time a trained VAE generates a new image.
exercises.explain: False! The encoder is only needed during training to help shape the latent space. To generate new data afterward, we skip the encoder entirely and use only the decoder.

exercises.sentence: After training, a VAE can generate new data by feeding a random latent vector directly to the ____.
exercises.explain: The decoder is the part that transforms latent vectors into realistic outputs, so it's the only piece needed for generating new samples.

exercises.prompt: Which of these best describes 'generating' with a trained VAE?

options.text: Skipping the encoder and giving random latent numbers straight to the decoder
Running the encoder on a blank white image first
Copying a training image pixel by pixel

exercises.explain: Generating means starting from a random point in latent space and letting the decoder turn it into something new, no encoder or copying involved.

exercises.prompt: A student says: 'To make a new cartoon character with our trained VAE, we must first draw a real character and encode it.' What's wrong with this thinking, and what should they do instead?

options.text: Nothing is wrong; encoding a real character is required to generate anything new
They should skip the encoder, pick random numbers as a latent vector, and feed them to the decoder to get a brand new character
They should retrain the entire VAE from scratch every time they want a new character
They should feed the decoder's output back into itself repeatedly

exercises.explain: Since the decoder already learned how to turn latent vectors into realistic outputs, the student doesn't need a real starting image at all. Just choose random numbers as the latent vector and pass them to the decoder to get a fresh, original character.
